title: Finding Registry Entry
description: Schema for one JSONL entry in .agents/findings/registry.jsonl. Each line represents one reusable structured finding in the canonical intake ledger that later promotion, planning, judgment, or compiler phases can reload.

id.description: Stable identifier for this finding entry.

version.description: Contract version for this finding entry.

tier.description: Whether the finding was authored locally, seeded, or pulled from another repo.

source.description: Origin metadata that lets readers trace the finding back to the source artifact.

date.description: Date when the finding was normalized into the registry.

severity.description: Severity label used for reader ranking.

category.description: Normalized category slug for grouping related findings.

pattern.description: Short natural-language description of the reusable failure pattern.

detection_question.description: Prompt a later planner or judge can ask to detect recurrence.

checklist_item.description: Concrete preventive action to add to planning or review.

applicable_languages.description: Language or artifact hints used by readers when ranking findings.

applicable_when.description: Controlled-vocabulary work-shape tags used to decide when the finding should be injected.

status.description: Only active entries are eligible for future injection.

superseded_by.description: Finding id that replaced this entry when status is superseded.

dedup_key.description: Canonical merge key used to coalesce repeated observations of the same reusable failure.

hit_count.description: How many times this finding has been cited by later runs. Later v2 CLI/runtime flows may update this without changing the registry line shape.

last_cited.description: Most recent citation timestamp. Null is allowed until the first later citation.

ttl_days.description: Advisory expiration window. Auto-retirement may remain unimplemented, but the field is part of the canonical ledger contract.

confidence.description: Confidence label for the reusable finding.
